lines.en_lit: matters that are written in this topic matters that are not written in that context, from where do we derive
Rav Naḥman bar Yitzḥak said: derives a opening opening Rav Mesharshiyya said: “And keep the charge of the Lord, invalidates Rav Ashi says: For so I am commanded, invalidate

The Sages taught: “For so I am commanded” “as I commanded” “as God has commanded” “For so I am commanded,” acute mourning, eat “As I commanded,” at the time of the incident “As God commanded,” and not from my I am saying

Rabbi Yosei bar Ḥanina said: Trousers are not written in the portion. When says: “And this is the matter that you shall do for them to sanctify them for service” to include trousers and the tenth of an ephah

Granted, trousers is written of the matter of garments However, the tenth of an ephah, from where do we derived this this “This is the offering of Aaron and of his sons, which they shall offer to the Lord a tenth of an ephah”

Rabbi Yoḥanan said in the name of Rabbi Shimon ben Yoḥai: From where that even read the portion invalidates The verse states: “And Moses said to the assembly: This is the matter [davar] that God has commanded even recitation [dibbur] invalidates
How, dress In what order did he dress them? what was, was. Rather, How dress in the future, In the future, too, when Aaron and his sons will come and Moses with them,

Rather, How dress them? to explain the verses The sons of Rabbi Ḥiyya and Rabbi Yoḥanan disagree with regard to One said: Aaron and afterward his sons; and one said: Aaron and his sons simultaneously,

Abaye said: tunic and mitre everyone agrees that Aaron and afterward his sons, as both in the command and in the implementation, Aaron precedes When they disagree, with regard to the belt. one who said: Aaron and afterward his sons which is written: “And he girded him with the belt” and then it is written: “And he girded them with belts” And one who said: Aaron and his sons, simultaneously which is written: “And gird them And to the one who says: Aaron and his sons simultaneously, isn’t it written: He girded him with the belt, and then it is written: He girded them with belts,
